operating_state: Total sales and revenues decreased 4% to $16.689 billion in Q2 2024, driven by lower sales volume of $1.206 billion partially offset by favorable price realization of $578 million. The volume decline primarily reflected dealer inventory decreases during Q2 2024 versus increases in Q2 2023. Order backlog reached $28.6 billion at quarter-end, up $0.7 billion from Q1 2024. Consolidated operating cash flow increased $251 million to $5.07 billion in the first six months driven by higher profit before taxes and lower working capital requirements.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin compressed to 20.9% from 21.1% in Q2 2023, though adjusted operating profit margin improved to 22.4% from 21.3%. Favorable price realization was more than offset by lower sales volume profit impact, higher restructuring costs, and increased expenses from strategic initiative investments. Capital expenditures increased with full-year 2024 expectations at $2.0-2.5 billion.
material_events: The company divested two non-U.S. entities during Q2 2024, generating restructuring costs with full-year 2024 restructuring costs expected to reach approximately $450 million. The Board approved an 8 percent quarterly dividend increase to $1.41 per share and authorized an additional $20.0 billion share repurchase program, bringing total remaining authorization to $21.6 billion. The company repurchased $6.28 billion of common stock in the first six months of 2024.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring supply chain disruptions, inflationary cost pressures, and labor constraints across transportation, components, and raw materials. Weak economic conditions persist in Europe and Asia Pacific outside China, while China excavator demand above 10-tons remains at relatively low levels. Mixed construction activity continues across Latin America alongside ongoing exposure to commodity price changes, international trade policies, and currency fluctuations.
forward_outlook: Management expects slightly lower full-year 2024 sales versus 2023, with second-half revenues slightly higher than first-half following normal seasonal patterns and machine dealer inventories anticipated to decrease slightly. Services revenues expected to grow in second-half 2024, while price realization benefits will significantly diminish as favorable 2023 trends normalize. The company maintains its strategy to return substantially all free cash flow to shareholders over time while maintaining its mid-A credit rating.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing supply chain challenges that may impact customer demand fulfillment capabilities, with dealer inventory management subject to independent dealer expectations regarding future demand and macroeconomic conditions. Energy transition dynamics create both commodity demand opportunities and market timing uncertainties across customer capital discipline decisions. The company maintains global credit facilities with total debt decreasing to $37.31 billion and cash position at $4.34 billion.
